title: Pseudo indexer service for two-level project indexing with FTS and prose overlay
purpose: Provides core indexing functionality for project source files including structural scanning, prose overlay application, FTS indexing, and snapshot writing with scan run tracking and error handling.
module_context: Exports ScanTrigger type, ScanOptions interface, ScanRun interface, and PseudoIndexer interface. Defines constants STUB_BYTE_LIMIT and STUB_LINE_LIMIT. Includes helper functions for hashing, timestamping, error detection, language detection, and file listing. Contains database statement preparation and execution functions for scan operations.

steps.content: Compute the SHA1 hash of the input content.
Return the hash as a hexadecimal string.
Generate the current timestamp in ISO format.
Return the timestamp string.
Check if the error is an instance of Error.
IF the error is an Error, check if its name is 'AbortError' or code is 'ABORT_ERR'.
Return true if either condition is met, otherwise false.
Convert the file path to lowercase for comparison.
IF the path ends with .ts or .tsx, return 'ts'.
ELSE IF the path ends with .js, .jsx, or .mjs, return 'js'.
ELSE IF the path ends with .py, return 'py'.
ELSE IF the path ends with .cs, return 'cs'.
ELSE return 'unknown'.
Initialize an empty output array for prose file paths.
IF the prose root directory does not exist, return the empty array.
Initialize a stack with the prose root directory.
While the stack is not empty, pop a directory from the stack.
For each entry in the directory, check if it is a file or directory.
IF the entry is a directory, push it onto the stack.
ELSE IF the entry is a file ending in .json and not named _path_map.json, add its full path to the output array.
Return the list of prose file paths.
Prepare and return a set of database statements for inserting method data.
Prepare statement for inserting method steps.
Prepare statement for inserting method calls.
Prepare statement for inserting file imports.
Prepare statement for inserting file records.
Prepare statement for inserting stub file records.
Prepare statement for updating file heuristic data.

methods.name: recordScanError
methods.normalized_params: (ctx: ScanContext, filePath: string, phase: string, err: unknown): void

steps.content: Increment the error count in the scan context.
Attempt to insert the error into the scan_errors table with scan run ID, file path, error message, and phase.
IF the insert fails, log a warning about failing to record the scan error.
Delete all rows from the files table matching the specified file path.
Read the file content as a buffer from the absolute path.
IF reading fails, record the error and return null.
Compute the SHA1 hash of the file content.
Count the number of lines in the file.
IF the file exceeds size or line limits, insert a stub file record and return a stub outcome.
Decode the buffer to UTF-8 string content.
IF decoding fails, record the error and return null.
Perform a structural scan of the source file.
IF the scan fails, record the error and return null.
Insert the file record into the database.
IF the insert fails, record the error and return null.
Extract docstrings from the file content.
IF docstring extraction fails, record the error and continue with an empty map.
Process each method in the scan result.
Compute the method ID and body fingerprint for each method.
Insert the method record into the database.
IF the insert fails, record the error and continue to the next method.
IF the method has docstring steps, insert them into the database.
IF inserting steps fails, record the error.
IF the method has call edges, insert them into the database.
IF inserting calls fails, record the error.
IF the file has imports, insert them into the database.
IF inserting imports fails, record the error.
Extract title and purpose from the file's docstrings.
IF title or purpose is found, update the file heuristic data in the database.
IF updating fails, record the error.
Return the scan outcome including file path, method rows, method names, step contents, title, purpose, and stub status.
Prepare database statements for updating method prose data, deleting method steps, inserting method steps, and inserting overlay matches.
For each match in the overlay, update the method's prose origin, match quality, and warning.
Delete existing steps for the matched method.
Insert the new steps from the overlay prose.
Insert an overlay match record for the method.
For each orphan in the overlay, insert an orphan prose record.
Ensure the FTS map table exists in the database.
For each scan outcome, skip stub files.
Fetch method step contents for the file from the database.
IF fetching fails, log a warning and continue.
Fetch the file title and purpose from the database.
IF fetching fails, continue with default values.
Upsert the file FTS record with title, purpose, step content, and method names.
Initialize an empty map for prose files.
Construct the prose root directory path.
List all prose files in the root directory.
For each prose file, attempt to read and parse it.
IF reading fails, log a warning and continue.
Store the parsed prose file in the map with its path as key.
Return the map of prose files.
Insert a new scan run record with the trigger and status 'running'.
Return the ID of the newly inserted scan run.

methods.name: finishScanRun
methods.normalized_params: (db: Database, id: number, status: 'done' | 'failed' | 'cancelled', filesScanned: number, errors: number, errorMsg: string | null): ScanRun

steps.content: Update the scan run record with the final status, finish timestamp, files scanned count, error count, and error message.
Fetch and return the updated scan run record.
Initialize scan in flight flag and abort controller.
Define a combined signal function that creates or reuses an abort controller.
Define a check abort function that throws an error if the signal is aborted.
Define the runFullScan function that performs a complete project scan.
Define the runIncrementalScan function that performs an incremental scan for specified paths.
Define the runIncrementalScanForFile function that wraps incremental scan for a single file.
Define the runReranking function as a stub.
Define the runOrphanDetection function as a stub.
Define the cancel function that aborts the current scan.
Return an object containing all defined functions as the pseudo indexer interface.
IF a scan is already in progress, throw an error.
Set the scan in flight flag to true.
Create a combined abort signal.
Insert a new scan run record into the database.
Initialize error count and files scanned counter.
Prepare database statements for scan operations.
Begin a database transaction.
Delete existing overlay matches, orphan prose, file imports, method calls, method steps, methods, and files.
Clear the FTS index.
Walk the project and scan each file.
Load all prose files from the project.
Overlay prose on methods using loaded prose files.
Apply the overlay results to the database.
Resolve call edges in the database.
Populate FTS for scanned files.
Commit the database transaction.
Run reranking and orphan detection.
Finish the scan run with success status.
Write a snapshot of the database.
Return the scan run result.
IF an error occurs during the scan, rollback the transaction and re-throw the error.
IF an abort occurs, finish the scan run with cancelled status.
Reset the scan in flight flag and abort controller.
IF a scan is already in progress, throw an error.
Set the scan in flight flag to true.
Create a combined abort signal.
Insert a new scan run record into the database.
Initialize error count and files scanned counter.
Prepare database statements for scan operations.
Begin a database transaction.
For each path, delete existing file rows and FTS entries.
Scan each file in the paths.
Load relevant prose files for the target paths.
Overlay prose on methods using relevant prose files.
Apply the overlay results to the database.
Resolve call edges for the scope files.
Populate FTS for scanned files.
Commit the database transaction.
Finish the scan run with success status.
Return the scan run result.
IF an error occurs during the scan, rollback the transaction and re-throw the error.
IF an abort occurs, finish the scan run with cancelled status.
Reset the scan in flight flag and abort controller.
Call the runIncrementalScan function with the path wrapped in an array.
Stub implementation for pseudo-ranking wave.

methods.name: runOrphanDetection
methods.normalized_params: (_opts: ScanOptions): Promise<{ crossBranch: string[]; actualOrphans: string[] }>

steps.content: Stub implementation for pseudo-orphan wave.
Return empty arrays for crossBranch and actualOrphans.
Abort the current scan using the abort controller.
